spectrum¶

Spectra¶

Per-spectrum loading and redshift transformation.

useSpec() is the main entry point: it reads a spectrum from disk (or from a metadata-driven path), applies Galactic extinction correction, optionally converts air wavelengths to vacuum, and shifts flux to the stacking redshift frame applying flux conservation scaling.

spectraPyle.spectrum.spectra.useSpec(config, specid, z, zStack, ebv_gal, cosmo, grism, metadata_name=None, hdu_indx=None)[source]¶

Load and transform a spectrum to the stacking redshift frame.

This function reads a spectrum using either standard or metadata-driven I/O depending on config[‘spectra_datafile’], then applies optional Galactic extinction correction, air-to-vacuum conversion, and flux / luminosity conservation scaling to a target stacking redshift.

Parameters:

  • config (dict) – Configuration dictionary containing instrument and processing options.

  • specid (str or int) – Spectrum identifier.

  • z (float) – Source redshift.

  • zStack (float) – Target stacking redshift.

  • ebv_gal (float) – Galactic extinction E(B-V).

  • cosmo (astropy.cosmology) – Cosmology object used for luminosity distance calculations.

  • grism (str) – Grism identifier

  • metadata_name (str, optional) – Metadata file path (required if spectra_datafile=’metadata’).

  • hdu_indx (int, optional) – FITS HDU index (required if metadata mode).

Returns:

lbd_zStackndarray

Wavelengths shifted to stacking redshift frame.

flux_zStackndarray

Flux scaled to stacking redshift frame.

error_zStackndarray

Flux error scaled to stacking redshift frame.

Return type:

tuple

Raises:

  • NameError – If unsupported conservation mode is requested.

  • ValueError – If metadata mode is enabled but metadata inputs are missing.

Notes

Conservation modes: - ‘luminosity’ : converts flux preserving luminosity - ‘flux’ : rescales flux by redshift factor only - ‘none’ : leaves flux unchanged

Normalization¶

Flux normalization for stacking.

Provides multiple normalization strategies controlled by config['norm_type'], including median-flux normalization over a wavelength range, and the francis1991_normalize() template-based method (Francis et al. 1991).

spectraPyle.spectrum.normalization.francis1991_normalize(stackArr, stackArrErr, min_overlap=50, norm_stat='median', sigma_clip=3.0, max_iter_clip=3, feature_mask=None, eps=1e-10)[source]¶

Robust Francis-style normalization with sigma-clipped overlap scaling.

Parameters:

  • stackArr (ndarray (Npix, Nspec)) – Flux array

  • stackArrErr (ndarray (Npix, Nspec)) – 1-sigma errors

  • min_overlap (int) – Minimum overlap for normalization

  • norm_stat ({'median', 'mean'}) – Statistic used after clipping

  • feature_mask (ndarray (Npix,) or None) – True = exclude from normalization

  • sigma_clip (float) – Sigma clipping threshold (default: 3)

  • max_iter_clip (int) – Max iterations for clipping

  • eps (float) – Small value to avoid division issues

Returns:

  • norm_flux (ndarray)

  • norm_err (ndarray)

spectraPyle.spectrum.normalization.normSpecCustom(lbd, flux, error, norm)[source]¶

Normalize a spectrum by a caller-supplied scalar value.

The normalization scalar norm is typically read from a catalog column (e.g. a photometric flux in a reference band). Use spectra_normalization: "custom" in the configuration and set catalog_columns.custom_normalization.custom_column_name to the column name.

Parameters:

  • lbd (array-like) – Wavelength array (Å). Accepted for API consistency but not used.

  • flux (ndarray) – Flux array.

  • error (ndarray) – Error (1-sigma) array.

  • norm (float) – Normalization scalar. Must be finite and > 0.

Returns:

  • fluxNorm (ndarray) – Normalized flux array.

  • errorNorm (ndarray) – Normalized error array.

  • norm (float) – The normalization value applied.

Raises:

ValueError – If norm is not finite or is ≤ 0.

spectraPyle.spectrum.normalization.normSpecIntegrMean(lbd, flux, error)[source]¶

Normalize spectrum by integrated mean flux.

Parameters:

  • lbd (ndarray) – Wavelength array.

  • flux (ndarray) – Flux array.

  • error (ndarray) – Flux error array.

Returns:

  • fluxNormndarray

    Normalized flux

  • errNormndarray

    Normalized error

  • normfloat

    Integrated mean flux value

Return type:

tuple

spectraPyle.spectrum.normalization.normSpecInterv(specid, lbd, flux, error, lambdamin_norm, lambdamax_norm, norm_stat)[source]¶

Normalize spectrum over a wavelength interval.

Parameters:

  • specid (str or int) – Spectrum identifier (for error messages).

  • lbd (ndarray) – Wavelength array.

  • flux (ndarray) – Flux array.

  • error (ndarray) – Flux error array.

  • lambdamin_norm (float) – Minimum wavelength of normalization interval (Å).

  • lambdamax_norm (float) – Maximum wavelength of normalization interval (Å).

  • norm_stat ({'median', 'mean', 'maximum', 'minimum'}) – Statistic to compute over the interval.

Returns:

  • fluxNormndarray

    Normalized flux

  • errNormndarray

    Normalized error

  • normfloat

    Normalization value used

Return type:

tuple

Raises:

ValueError – If normalization interval is outside wavelength range or contains no valid data.

spectraPyle.spectrum.normalization.normSpecMed(lbd, flux, error)[source]¶

Normalize spectrum by median flux.

Parameters:

  • lbd (ndarray) – Wavelength array (unused, for API consistency).

  • flux (ndarray) – Flux array.

  • error (ndarray) – Flux error array.

Returns:

  • fluxNormndarray

    Normalized flux

  • errNormndarray

    Normalized error

  • normfloat

    Median flux value used for normalization

Return type:

tuple

Resampling¶

Wavelength grid resampling.

Resamples individual spectra onto the common stacking wavelength grid using linear or logarithmic binning, as configured by config['pixel_resampling_type'].

spectraPyle.spectrum.resampling.dlam_from_R(R, lambda_ref, nsamp=5)[source]¶

Δλ (Å) from resolving power at a reference wavelength.

Parameters:

  • R (float) – Spectral resolving power (λ / Δλ_FWHM)

  • lambda_ref (float) – Reference wavelength (Å)

  • nsamp (float, optional) – Number of pixels per resolution element (default: 5)

Returns:

dlam – Linear wavelength step (Å)

Return type:

float

spectraPyle.spectrum.resampling.dloglam_from_R(R, nsamp=5)[source]¶

Compute logarithmic wavelength step Δlog10(λ) from resolving power.

Parameters:

  • R (float) – Spectral resolving power (λ / Δλ_FWHM)

  • nsamp (float, optional) – Number of pixels per resolution element (default: 5)

Returns:

dloglam – Logarithmic wavelength step Δlog10(λ)

Return type:

float

spectraPyle.spectrum.resampling.dloglam_from_dlam(dlam, lambda_ref)[source]¶

Compute logarithmic wavelength step Δlog10(λ) from resolving power.

Δlog10𝜆=Δ𝜆/𝜆_ref*ln10

Parameters:

  • dlam (float) – Linear wavelength step (Å)

  • lambda_ref (float) – Reference wavelength (Å)

Returns:

dloglam – Logarithmic wavelength step Δlog10(λ)

Return type:

float

spectraPyle.spectrum.resampling.resamplingSpecFluxCons(lbd, flux, var, lambdaInterp)[source]¶

Fully vectorized 1D resampling with variance propagation. Adapted from Fruchter & Hook 2002 Produces flux density per unit wavelength.

Parameters:

  • lbd ((N_in,) array) – Input wavelength pixel centers

  • flux ((N_in,) array) – Flux density per input pixel

  • var ((N_in,) array) – Variance per input pixel

  • lambdaInterp ((N_out+1,) array) – Output wavelength bin edges

Returns:

  • flux_out ((N_out,) array) – flux density (per unit wavelength) over the new common wavelength grid

  • err_out ((N_out,) array) – error density (per unit wavelength) over the new common wavelength grid

spectraPyle.spectrum.resampling.resamplingSpecFluxCons_v0(lbd, flux, lambdaInterp)[source]¶

Resample a step-function spectrum while conserving flux.

Parameters:

  • lbd (ndarray) – Original wavelength array (pixel centers).

  • flux (ndarray) – Original flux array.

  • lambdaInterp (ndarray) – New wavelength grid (bin edges, length N+1).

Returns:

Resampled flux array (length N).

Return type:

ndarray
